steps.explanation: The figure shows right triangle QRS with the right angle at R. The side SR = 17 is given. We need QS, the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle at R, connecting Q to S).
For angle Q: the opposite side is SR = 17, and the hypotenuse is QS. The sine ratio gives $\sin Q = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}} = \frac{17}{QS}$, so $QS = \frac{17}{\sin Q}$.
The condition QR < RS confirms Q is the larger acute angle (the side opposite Q, which is RS, is longer), consistent with using sin Q here.

• A $17\cos Q$: uses cosine and multiplies — this would give the adjacent leg QR, not QS. Eliminated.
  • B $17\sin Q$: multiplying gives a value smaller than 17, but QS is the hypotenuse (longest side). Eliminated.
  • C $\frac{17}{\cos Q}$: this would apply if 17 were the adjacent side, which it is not. Eliminated.
  • D $\frac{17}{\sin Q}$: correctly derived from $\sin Q = 17/QS$. That matches choice D — D is correct.
